_comment: WhisperWard curated ASN authority file. This is an authority, not a reference list. The enrichment engine asserts only the categories known-vpn and hosting-datacenter, spelled exactly. Any other category, or a missing one, makes the pipeline refuse to start in strict mode, which is the default. After every edit run python update_threat_lists.py to validate this file before your next case. The enricher reads only asn, organization, category, and source. The confidence field is analyst-only context and is ignored by the engine. These entries are seed candidates and should each be reviewed before being treated as settled authority, since ASN ownership and provider roles change over time. Do not add an ASN more than once, the loader keys on asn and a later duplicate silently overwrites the earlier one.

asns.source: major cloud hosting provider, frequently observed origin for automated and hosted traffic
major cloud compute provider, hosts both benign and abusive workloads
major cloud compute provider, hosts both benign and abusive workloads
major cloud compute provider, hosts both benign and abusive workloads
major cloud compute provider, hosts both benign and abusive workloads

European hosting provider, common automated traffic origin

hosting provider, mixed-use infrastructure, review per case
network heavily used by commercial VPN exit infrastructure, frequently observed as VPN egress
network associated with commercial VPN exit infrastructure, verify per case
